summary: Enemy forces have adapted by switching to new frequencies and expanding the "kill zone" by tens of kilometers, with FPV drones now targeting logistics near Donetsk and operating in a coordinated "carousel" near Peski, just 25-30 km from the DPR capital. The Rubikon Center reported 162 targets destroyed on March 10, including strikes on Ukrainian personnel and FPV air defense operations against fixed-wing UAVs. Electronic warfare struggles to counter Lightning drones due to unknown control frequencies, while the decline in BM-35 and BM-70 drone attacks may be linked to production issues, increased electronic warfare, and the disconnection of Starlink systems. On the ground, a Russian soldier was hit by two Ukrainian FPV drones simultaneously, and an American MaxxPro armored vehicle was destroyed in Dobropole by a fiber-optic FPV drone.
